svg.text: Boyer-Moore — Right-to-Left Scan with Two Heuristics
• Align pattern P with text T at position i. Compare P right-to-left against T[i..i+m-1].
• On mismatch (or full match), shift i forward by max(bad-character shift, good-suffix shift).
• Right-to-left scan lets shifts skip past entire blocks of text — sublinear in practice.
When T[i+j] = c mismatches P[j], shift so the rightmost occurrence of c in P aligns with i+j.
If c never appears in P, shift past the entire pattern: i ← i + j + 1.
Preprocessing: O(σ + m). Table size σ (alphabet) — for ASCII just 256 ints.
After matching suffix P[j+1..m-1] but mismatching P[j], shift so the matched suffix aligns
with its next-rightmost occurrence in P (preceded by a different character).
If no such occurrence, shift so a prefix of P matches a suffix of the matched region.
Preprocessing: O(m) — analogous to KMP failure function but on suffixes.
Galil's rule: after a full match, also use good-suffix to bound re-comparisons → linear worst case.
Combined shift = max(bad-char, good-suffix). Both safe — never miss a match.
Average O(n/m); worst O(n·m) without Galil, O(n) with Galil.
